title: When My Habits Crashed (And How I Came Back)

content.setup: Before my nephew was born, my sister had this perfect morning routine. Exercise, journaling, healthy breakfast - she was a habit machine. Then the baby came.
content.development: For three months, she had ONE habit: survival. She felt like a complete failure. All those routines she'd built? Gone. She called me crying, saying she'd 'lost herself.'
content.turning_point: I reminded her: those neural pathways didn't disappear. They're just dormant. And right now, her ONE job was to keep herself and the baby alive. That's it. Everything else could wait.
content.lesson: Life transitions don't erase your habits - they pause them. The comeback is always possible. But you have to give yourself permission to let go first.
content.application: If you're in a major life transition and your habits have crashed, hear me: this is temporary. When you're ready, we'll rebuild. No shame.
